name: firebase-development
description: This skill should be used when working with Firebase projects, including initializing projects, adding Cloud Functions or Firestore collections, debugging emulator issues, or reviewing Firebase code. Triggers on "firebase", "firestore", "cloud functions", "emulator", "firebase auth", "deploy to firebase", "firestore rules".
capabilities: Initialize new Firebase projects with TypeScript, emulators, and testing setup
Add Cloud Functions, Firestore collections, and Express API endpoints using TDD
Debug emulator port conflicts, rules violations, and deployment failures
Validate Firebase code against security best practices and architecture patterns
author: 2389-research
securitySummary: This is a documentation/guidance orchestration skill with no executable code. All 302 static findings are false positives - the skill contains documentation examples of Firebase CLI commands, not actual command execution vulnerabilities. Patterns flagged (external commands, network access, credential references) are inherent to Firebase development documentation.
valueStatement: Firebase projects require specific architecture decisions and CLI workflows. This skill guides you through initializing projects, adding Cloud Functions, debugging emulators, and validating security rules with proven patterns from production apps.
userTitle: Set up and develop Firebase projects
limitations: Does not execute Firebase CLI commands - provides guidance only
Does not modify your project files directly
Does not deploy to Firebase - recommends deployment commands
Does not access external Firebase services

useCases.title: New Firebase project setup
useCases.description: Initialize a new Firebase project with TypeScript functions, emulators, and proper architecture patterns.

useCases.title: Add Cloud Functions
useCases.description: Add new API endpoints or Firestore triggers following TDD workflow and security best practices.

useCases.title: Debug Firebase issues
useCases.description: Troubleshoot emulator startup problems, rules violations, and deployment failures systematically.

promptTemplates.title: Initialize project
promptTemplates.prompt: I want to start a new Firebase project. Help me set it up with TypeScript, Cloud Functions, Firestore, and emulators.

promptTemplates.title: Add feature
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a new HTTP endpoint to my Firebase project that creates user profiles in Firestore.

promptTemplates.title: Debug issue
promptTemplates.prompt: My Firebase emulators are not starting. Getting a port conflict error. Help me debug this.

promptTemplates.title: Validate code
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my Firebase project for security issues. Check the Firestore rules and authentication implementation.

bestPractices: Always use Firebase emulators during local development before deploying
Follow server-write-only security model for light-write applications
Write tests first using TDD before implementing any feature
Use TypeScript for all Cloud Functions with proper type safety
antiPatterns: Deploying without testing with emulators first
Using client-write rules without proper validation (missing diff().affectedKeys())
Mixing Express, Domain-Grouped, and Individual function architectures
Skipping ABOUTME comments on function files

faq.question: Does this skill execute Firebase CLI commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides step-by-step guidance on what commands to run. You execute the commands yourself.

faq.question: What architecture patterns are supported?
faq.answer: Three patterns: Express API (middleware, routing), Domain-Grouped (feature areas), and Individual files (maximum modularity).

faq.question: How does the routing work?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes your request for keywords like 'init', 'add function', 'debug', or 'review' and routes to the appropriate sub-skill. If intent is unclear, it asks you to choose from 4 options.

faq.question: What security model should I use?
faq.answer: Prefer server-write-only for light-write applications. Only use client-write with validation for high-volume real-time apps.

faq.question: Can this skill review my existing Firebase code?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the validate sub-skill to audit configuration, rules, architecture, authentication, and production readiness.

faq.question: Does this skill handle Firebase Authentication?
faq.answer: It guides implementation of both API Key authentication and Firebase Auth + roles patterns with proper security checks.
